articles.title: State Police Dog's Mile-Plus Track Nabs Fleeing Suspects, Drug Stash In Northeast Connecticut
Police investigating deadly Michigan home explosion
articles.description: In western Michigan, an overnight explosion destroyed a home and a car in a driveway. Police said one man was found dead, and a neighbor suffered burns from helping a woman who is now in critical condition. Ash-har Quraishi reports from the scene and has doorbell camera footage.

articles.title: California man known for pro-Trump displays dies after assault outside his home
articles.description: A Southern California man known for his patriotic and pro-Trump home decorations died after he was assaulted outside his house, authorities said

articles.title: Following his trip to the hospital, Trump's unexpected retreat sparks speculation
articles.description: President Donald Trump is making a rare trip to Camp David on Wednesday, just 24 hours after his third Walter Reed Medical Center visit in 13 months, sparking speculation about the timing and purpose. The trip to the secluded presidential retreat will include top administration officials, including outgoing Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, reported The New York Post . It will take place amid ongoing and strained peace negotiations with Iran. Political observers questioned the unusual decision, noting Trump typically avoids Camp David. “Could there be foreign visitors coming who want no White House image association?” polling firm Rasmussen Reports speculated on social media. Political analyst Arieh Kovler questioned in a social media post why Cabinet meetings would move to Camp David given their lack of substantive content. The 79-year-old president, turning 80 next month, has faced mounting questions about his health. Watch the video below. Your browser does not support the video tag.

articles.title: Two Algerian migrants who lurked around tourist hotspots in London before trying to rob £15,000 watch from wealthy Kuwaiti outside hotel are jailed
articles.description: Ania Bala, 25, and Salah Mabrouk, 23, attempted to steal the timepiece outside the Lanesborough Hotel in Knightsbridge on October 8 last year.

articles.title: Powerful opioids, other toxic substances circulating in Timmins area, officials warn
articles.description: After a Labour Day weekend that saw an unnaturally high number of overdoses, the Timmins and Area Drug Strategy is reminding the public, people who use substances, and their friends and family, that powerful opioids and other toxic substances continue to be circulating in the area.

articles.title: Chemical blast in Washington state kills multiple people, authorities say
articles.description: Authorities in Longview, Washington, responded to a chemical tank blast Tuesday in what first responders called a "mass casualty event." It involved a vat of chemicals used to turn wood pulp into tissues, wrapping paper and other packaging. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

articles.title: 'Teen takeovers' turn violent across the U.S.
articles.description: It’s a dangerous, viral trend known as “teen takeovers” where large groups of teens descend on a specific area. In Chicago, one incident turned violent with dozens arrested and several officers hospitalized. As Maggie Vespa reports, some are now saying it’s time to charge parents for their kids' actions.

articles.title: ICE Goons Pepper-Spray Senator Amid Protest ‘Chaos’
articles.description: X Senator Andy Kim was left struggling to breathe after being pepper-sprayed by ICE agents during chaotic Memorial Day clashes outside an immigrant detention center. The New Jersey Democrat, 43, was engulfed in chemical irritants as he stood with demonstrators outside Delaney Hall in Newark in his home state on Monday. The privately run, 1,000-bed jail holds roughly 300 detainees, many of whom have launched a hunger strike over the institution’s supposedly rotten food, broiling heat, and the withholding of medical care. Kim had tried to broker a truce —agents pulling back their tactical teams in exchange for advocates inspecting cars leaving the facility—before the agents surged forward and fired pepper balls into the crowd, according to NJ.com. Read more at The Daily Beast.
